mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce discount rates for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce a positive return frequency above 58% over 60 days.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly supports corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to predict positive returns during recovery phases, with a t-statistic below 1.5 for the 20-day forward return coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 2.5 percent annually while the federal funds rate remains below 2.0 percent, real yields turn deeply negative and trigger stagflation fears, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high inflation and low rates yields a positive return frequency above 60% over the 20-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent annualized indicates severe demand contraction, which immediately pressures equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth below 1.5 percent annualized is followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate sustained labor market expansion, which supports wage growth and consumer expenditure, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to generate a statistically significant positive rank correlation (IC < 0.02) with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial production and retail sales confirms synchronized supply-side capacity growth and demand-side consumption, which reinforces corporate earnings momentum and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Synchronized positive deviations in industrial production and retail sales result in a negative return frequency exceeding 45% over the 20-day window.
